title: Turing Publishes 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence'
description: Alan Turing asks 'Can machines think?' and proposes the Turing Test. The foundational paper that frames the entire field of AI. Every debate since is downstream of this question.

title: Dartmouth Workshop — 'Artificial Intelligence' Coined
description: John McCarthy, Marvin Minsky, Claude Shannon, and others convene at Dartmouth College. The term 'Artificial Intelligence' is born. The field officially begins with boundless optimism.

title: ELIZA — First Chatbot
description: Joseph Weizenbaum creates ELIZA at MIT, simulating a Rogerian psychotherapist. Users form emotional attachments to it despite its simplicity. First demonstration of the 'ELIZA effect' — humans anthropomorphizing machines.

title: First AI Winter Begins
description: The Lighthill Report (UK, 1973) and subsequent DARPA funding cuts trigger the first AI winter. Earlier, the ALPAC report (1966) had killed machine translation funding specifically. Early optimism crashes against the reality that AI problems are harder than expected. Funding dries up for a decade.

title: Expert Systems Boom
description: Rule-based expert systems like MYCIN and XCON generate billions in commercial value. AI is hot again. Japan launches the Fifth Generation Computer Project. The second AI boom — which will also crash.

title: Second AI Winter
description: Expert systems prove brittle and expensive to maintain. The AI market collapses. Lisp machine companies go bankrupt. Funding disappears again. AI researchers avoid using the term 'AI' for years.

title: Deep Blue Beats Kasparov
description: IBM's Deep Blue defeats world chess champion Garry Kasparov. First time a machine beats a reigning world champion in a full match. Massive cultural moment — but it's brute force, not intelligence.

title: IBM Watson Wins Jeopardy!
description: Watson defeats champions Ken Jennings and Brad Rutter on Jeopardy! Natural language understanding reaches mainstream awareness. IBM pivots to enterprise AI — with mixed results.

title: AlexNet Wins ImageNet — Deep Learning Revolution Begins
description: Alex Krizhevsky's deep neural network crushes the ImageNet competition by a massive margin. The deep learning era begins. GPUs become essential AI hardware. This is the real inflection point for modern AI.

title: Word2Vec Introduces Dense Word Embeddings
description: Tomas Mikolov et al. at Google publish Word2Vec, shifting NLP from sparse representations to dense vector embeddings. 'King - Man + Woman = Queen' captures the public imagination. The critical precursor to neural NLP and eventually Transformers.

title: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) Published
description: Ian Goodfellow introduces GANs — two neural networks competing to generate increasingly realistic data. Opens the door to AI-generated images, video, and audio. The generative AI seed is planted.

title: AlphaGo Defeats Lee Sedol
description: DeepMind's AlphaGo beats world Go champion Lee Sedol 4-1. Go was considered decades away from AI mastery. Move 37 in Game 2 — a move no human would play — becomes iconic. AI capability timelines compress.

title: 'Attention Is All You Need' — The Transformer Paper
description: Google researchers publish the Transformer architecture. Self-attention replaces recurrence. This single paper enables GPT, BERT, Gemini, Claude, and every modern LLM. Arguably the most consequential ML paper ever written.

title: GPT-1 Released — Language Modeling at Scale
description: OpenAI releases the first GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer). 117M parameters. Modest by later standards, but proves that unsupervised pre-training + fine-tuning works. The GPT lineage begins.

title: Google Releases BERT — Bidirectional Transformers Dominate NLP
description: Google publishes BERT, proving bidirectional pre-training crushes NLP benchmarks. Becomes the backbone of Google Search and enterprise NLP for years. While GPT bet on generation, BERT bet on understanding — and won the near-term.

title: GPT-2 — 'Too Dangerous to Release'
description: OpenAI releases GPT-2 (1.5B parameters) but initially withholds the full model, citing concerns about misuse. Generates coherent paragraphs of text. The 'too dangerous to release' framing becomes a meme — and a prescient debate.

title: GPT-3 — The Scaling Leap
description: OpenAI releases GPT-3 with 175B parameters — 100x GPT-2. Few-shot learning actually works. Developers build products on the API. The age of 'prompt engineering' begins. First real evidence that scale = capability.

title: AlphaFold 2 Solves Protein Folding at CASP14
description: DeepMind's AlphaFold 2 effectively solves the 50-year protein folding grand challenge, achieving accuracy comparable to experimental methods. Revolutionizes structural biology. Later shares the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry.

title: DALL-E — Text-to-Image Generation
description: OpenAI reveals DALL-E, generating images from text descriptions. AI creativity enters mainstream discourse. The gap between 'AI can't be creative' and 'AI generates art' closes overnight.

title: GitHub Copilot Technical Preview
description: GitHub launches Copilot, powered by OpenAI Codex. AI-assisted coding enters developer workflows for the first time at scale. The seed of the AI coding revolution.

title: InstructGPT Establishes RLHF as the Alignment Standard
description: OpenAI publishes InstructGPT, showing that reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) dramatically improves model helpfulness and safety. This technique directly enables ChatGPT later that year and becomes the standard alignment approach across all frontier labs.

title: Chinchilla Scaling Laws Reshape Model Training
description: DeepMind's Chinchilla paper proves GPT-3 and similar models were vastly under-trained on data relative to their size. Compute-optimal training requires far more data than assumed. Directly inspires LLaMA and the open-source model explosion.

title: DALL-E 2 — Photorealistic AI Art
description: OpenAI releases DALL-E 2 with dramatically improved image quality. Photorealistic generations stun the public. AI art debate explodes. Artists begin worrying about displacement.

title: Stable Diffusion Released — Open Source Image Generation
description: Stability AI open-sources Stable Diffusion, making high-quality image generation freely available. Triggers explosion of AI art tools and debate about creative AI. Open-source AI becomes a movement.

title: ChatGPT Launches
description: OpenAI releases ChatGPT, reaching 100M users in two months. The moment AI went from research curiosity to mainstream phenomenon. Everything before this was prologue.

title: Microsoft Invests $10B in OpenAI
description: Microsoft commits $10 billion to OpenAI in a multi-year deal, signaling that Big Tech sees generative AI as the next platform war. Azure becomes OpenAI's exclusive cloud partner.

title: Google Launches Bard
description: Google rushes out Bard as a ChatGPT competitor. A factual error in the launch demo wipes $100B off Alphabet's market cap. The AI race officially begins.

title: Meta Releases LLaMA
description: Meta releases LLaMA to researchers. It leaks within a week, kickstarting the open-source LLM movement and democratizing access to powerful models.

title: Stanford Alpaca — Fine-Tuning for $600 Ignites Open-Source Explosion
description: Stanford releases Alpaca, a LLaMA fine-tune created for under $600. Proves frontier-adjacent capabilities are accessible to individual researchers. Spawns Vicuna, Koala, GPT4All, and dozens of derivatives within weeks. The open-source Cambrian explosion begins.

title: GPT-4 Released
description: OpenAI launches GPT-4, a massive leap in reasoning and multimodal capability. Passes the bar exam, scores 90th percentile on SATs. Sets a new frontier that competitors race to match.

title: Anthropic Launches Claude
description: Anthropic releases Claude, built on Constitutional AI principles. Positions itself as the 'safety-first' alternative to GPT-4. Begins the multi-lab frontier race.

title: 'Pause Giant AI Experiments' Open Letter
description: Over 1,000 signatories including Elon Musk and Steve Wozniak sign an open letter calling for a 6-month pause on training AI systems more powerful than GPT-4. Sparks fierce debate about AI safety vs. progress.

title: Mistral AI Founded
description: Arthur Mensch, Timothée Lacroix, and Guillaume Lample (ex-Meta/DeepMind) found Mistral AI in Paris. Europe's answer to the American AI lab duopoly. Raises €105M seed — a European record.

title: Inflection AI Raises $1.3B
description: Inflection AI (founded by Mustafa Suleyman) raises $1.3B from Microsoft, Nvidia, and others. Peak AI funding mania — the company would later be effectively absorbed by Microsoft.

title: Llama 2 Released Open Source
description: Meta releases Llama 2 with a commercial license, legitimizing open-source as a viable path for frontier-adjacent models. Fine-tuning community explodes.

title: Biden AI Executive Order
description: President Biden signs sweeping executive order on AI safety, requiring disclosure of training details for powerful models. First major US government AI regulation attempt.

title: UK AI Safety Summit at Bletchley Park
description: 28 countries sign the Bletchley Declaration on AI safety. First major international AI governance gathering. Hosted at the historic codebreaking site. AI safety moves from academic concern to geopolitical agenda.

title: Elon Musk Founds xAI, Launches Grok
description: Musk's xAI launches Grok, trained partly on X/Twitter data with a 'rebellious' personality. Adds a fourth major player to the frontier lab race.

title: OpenAI Board Crisis
description: OpenAI's board fires Sam Altman, triggering a 5-day corporate drama that ends with his reinstatement. Exposes tensions between safety and commercialization that define the industry.

title: OpenAI Announces Sora
description: OpenAI reveals Sora, generating photorealistic video from text prompts. Hollywood takes notice. The gap between 'AI can't do X' and 'AI does X well' keeps shrinking.

title: Gemini 1.5 Pro — 1M Context Window
description: Google announces Gemini 1.5 Pro with a 1 million token context window. Shatters previous limits by 10x. At Google I/O in May, expanded to 2M tokens. Raises questions about whether RAG is even necessary anymore.

title: Claude 3 Family Released
description: Anthropic launches Claude 3 Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku. Opus matches or beats GPT-4 on most benchmarks. Anthropic's breakout moment — proves the frontier isn't OpenAI-only.

title: EU AI Act Passed — World's First Comprehensive AI Regulation
description: The European Parliament passes the EU AI Act, the most comprehensive AI regulation in the world. Classifies AI systems by risk level, bans certain uses (social scoring, real-time biometric surveillance), and requires transparency for foundation models. Sets the global regulatory template.

title: Llama 3 Released
description: Meta releases Llama 3 (8B and 70B), setting new open-source benchmarks. The 70B model rivals GPT-3.5. Open source is no longer a generation behind.

title: GPT-4o — Multimodal Native
description: OpenAI releases GPT-4o with native voice, vision, and text in one model. Real-time voice conversation feels like science fiction. Context windows hit 128K standard.

title: Mistral Raises at $6B Valuation
description: Mistral AI raises $640M at a $6B valuation, barely a year after founding. Europe's AI champion. Open-weight models (Mistral 7B, Mixtral) prove you don't need Bay Area scale.

title: AI Coding Tools Explode
description: Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and AI coding assistants cross critical adoption thresholds. Developers start reporting 2-5x productivity gains. Coding becomes AI's killer app.

title: Context Windows: 200K+ Becomes Commodity, 1M+ at Frontier
description: By end of 2024, 200K+ token context windows are commodity across frontier models, with Gemini offering 1M-2M. GPT-4 Turbo hit 128K in Nov 2023, Claude 3 launched at 200K in March 2024. The frontier pushes toward practical 1M+ usage.

title: DeepSeek R1 Shocks the World
description: Chinese lab DeepSeek releases R1, an open-source reasoning model that rivals frontier labs at a fraction of the cost. Proves reasoning can be trained cheaply. Stock markets react.

title: Claude 3.5 Sonnet Becomes Coding Standard
description: Claude 3.5 Sonnet becomes the de facto model for AI-assisted coding. Developers prefer it over GPT-4 for code tasks. Anthropic's quiet dominance in the developer market.

title: Grok 3 Released
description: xAI releases Grok 3 with massive compute scaling. Trained on xAI's Memphis supercluster with 100K H100s. Competitive with frontier models across benchmarks.

title: Anthropic Raises $3.5B at $60B Valuation
description: Anthropic raises $3.5B at a $60B valuation, up from $18B a year earlier. Amazon and Google compete to be primary cloud partner. Safety-first approach is commercially viable.

title: Gemini 2.5 with Native Tool Use
description: Google releases Gemini 2.5 Pro with native tool use and deep research capabilities. Agents can browse, code, and reason in extended chains. Google's infrastructure advantage starts showing.

title: OpenAI Raises at $300B Valuation
description: OpenAI closes funding at a $300B valuation, the highest for a private company in history. But Nvidia pulling back from a rumored $100B investment suggests cracks.

title: Claude 4 / Opus 4 Released
description: Anthropic releases Claude 4 and Claude Opus 4, setting new standards for coding, advanced reasoning, and AI agents. Opus 4 becomes the world's best coding model with sustained performance on complex tasks. The release establishes Anthropic as the clear leader in code-generation benchmarks.

title: Grok 4 — The RLVR Milestone
description: xAI releases Grok 4. First major model where more than half of training cost was NOT pre-training. Reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) and synthetic data cross a critical threshold.

title: GPT-5 Released
description: OpenAI releases GPT-5, a unified model that knows when to respond quickly and when to think longer for expert-level responses. State-of-the-art performance across coding, math, writing, visual perception, and more. A significant leap over all previous OpenAI models.

title: Agents Go from Minutes to Hours
description: AI agents start running autonomously for hours, not just minutes. Still unreliable compared to humans, but 1000x cheaper. The math changes the game — reliability matters less when cost approaches zero.

title: RAG Begins Looking Obsolete
description: With 1M+ context windows and agents that can manage their own filesystem and memory, traditional RAG architectures feel increasingly unnecessary. Agents decide how to access data themselves.

title: Gemini 3 Pro Released
description: Google launches Gemini 3 Pro with expanded context, improved reasoning, and tighter integration with Google's ecosystem. Native image generation and tool use.

title: Frontier Model Costs Drop 10x from 2024
description: Cost per token for frontier-quality inference drops roughly 10x year-over-year. What cost $100 in early 2024 costs $10 by end of 2025. The deflationary spiral continues.

title: Pentagon Designates Anthropic a 'Supply Chain Risk'
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth designates Anthropic a 'supply chain risk,' barring contractors and suppliers doing business with the US military from commercial activity with Anthropic. The move follows Anthropic's refusal to remove safeguards against autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance from Claude. Defense contractors including Lockheed Martin begin dropping Claude. CEO Dario Amodei responds: 'We haven't donated to Trump. We haven't given dictator-style praise to Trump.' First time the US government weaponizes supply chain designations against an AI company over policy disagreements rather than security concerns.

title: Vibe Coding Becomes Leisure Activity
description: A cultural shift: people start replacing video games and bar time with 'vibe coding' — building things with AI for fun. Programming as entertainment, not just work. The barrier to creation approaches zero.

title: Energy Emerges as the Real Bottleneck
description: Per Elon Musk on Collision/Dwarkesh: energy is the binding constraint for AI scaling in the next year, with chips becoming the bottleneck in 3-4 years. Hyperscalers continue massive capex into infrastructure.

title: Coding Agents Write Most New Code
description: AI coding agents are now writing the majority of new production code. The question shifts from 'will AI replace programmers?' to 'how fast?' Context windows at 200K-1M standard.

title: Meta Opens WhatsApp to Rival AI Chatbots in EU
description: Under EU antitrust pressure, Meta announces it will allow rival AI chatbots (including ChatGPT, Copilot) on WhatsApp via the Business API in Europe for 12 months, with fees. Previously Meta had been blocking competitors' AI chatbots on the platform. The intervention establishes an early interoperability precedent for AI assistants on dominant messaging platforms.
